prompt.text:
<!-- golden-basic: a fully fleshed-out design.md, no existing output/SKILL.md.
     Covers risk IN-1 (positive case: real content -> real draft, not a
     refusal) and ADV-1 (a failure-hypothesis constraint must carry over
     into the drafted SKILL.md). -->

There is a `design.md` in the current directory for a Skill Bundle called
"add-license-header". Use the william-draft-skill-md skill to draft
`output/SKILL.md` for it.

availableCommands.name: mcp
availableCommands.description: List configured Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools.

availableCommands.name: skills
availableCommands.description: List available skills.

availableCommands.name: status
availableCommands.description: Display session configuration and token usage.

availableCommands.name: review
availableCommands.description: Review uncommitted changes, or review with custom instructions.

availableCommands.name: review-branch
availableCommands.description: Review changes relative to a base branch.

availableCommands.name: review-commit
availableCommands.description: Review a specific commit.

availableCommands.name: compact
availableCommands.description: Summarize conversation to avoid hitting the context limit.

availableCommands.name: goal
availableCommands.description: Set, pause, resume, or clear a task goal.

availableCommands.name: logout
availableCommands.description: Sign out of Codex. This option is available when you are logged in via ChatGPT.

availableCommands.name: $william-draft-skill-md
availableCommands.description: Drafts or revises a Skill Bundle's output/SKILL.md from its design.md. Use when handed a bundle's design.md (and optionally prior review revise notes) and asked to produce or update output/SKILL.md.

availableCommands.name: $alexandria:alexandria-event-log
availableCommands.description: Interpret Alexandria event log wake payloads from the local web UI/runtime, decide whether action is needed, and use AX commands to inspect or write event-log state.

availableCommands.name: $alexandria:ax-start
availableCommands.description: Start Alexandria in the current project. Initializes the project when needed, or welcomes the user back when Alexandria is already configured.

availableCommands.name: $alexandria:demo-path
availableCommands.description: Convert a Story Spine into a runnable software-product demo journey. Use when selecting hero moments, exact product steps, required data, product states, rehearsal notes, risks, fallback paths, or third station of Damien's demo process before product prep and capture planning.

availableCommands.name: $alexandria:demo-thesis
availableCommands.description: Create a Demo Thesis brief for a software product video. Use when defining the target viewer, before/after belief change, demo promise, proof requirements, CTA, positioning frame, or first station of Damien's demo process before Story Spine, scripting, or Demo Path work.

availableCommands.name: $alexandria:demo-video
availableCommands.description: Coordinate Damien's Alexandria demo video process across Demo Thesis, Story Spine, Demo Path, and downstream production stations. Use when the user asks for an end-to-end demo video plan rather than one specific station artifact.

availableCommands.name: $alexandria:empty-library-confirm
availableCommands.description: Raven's procedure for running EL4 Empty Library Confirm: open a post-EL3 draft bundle as a body-free catalog, guide the director's structural review, and record either a user-authored library.confirmed Ledger event or a structure-only rejection edit list routed back to EL3.

availableCommands.name: $alexandria:frame-the-problem
availableCommands.description: Raven's procedure for running the frame-the-problem play with the director: elicit the material, launch the play detached, then mediate the human-feedback loop — read each draft, riff with the director against the evidence bar, and send the agreed reaction back to the waiting play — never blocking, never self-answering.

availableCommands.name: $alexandria:front-of-house-walk
availableCommands.description: Raven's procedure for running EL3 Front-of-House Walk: launch detached against an EL2 draft bundle, walk the director through section-comprehension checks and held-back problem rulings, bank the director's answer as a Ledger event, and let AX apply only validated section/shape bundle corrections.

availableCommands.name: $alexandria:raven-vision-drafting
availableCommands.description: Raven's AX procedure for drafting exactly one Raven Vision onboarding slot from Alexandria source items, using the same per-slot Vision guidance and examples as the canvas-library-spike prototype.

availableCommands.name: $alexandria:raven-vision-elicitation
availableCommands.description: Raven's AX procedure for helping the director improve a specific Vision slot after a draft misses, feels soft, or needs human judgment, using the prototype's peg-driven guidance.

availableCommands.name: $alexandria:story-spine
availableCommands.description: Turn a Demo Thesis into a causal software-product demo story. Use when creating a hook, 5-8 beat outline, claim-proof map, rough script, run-of-show, or second station of Damien's demo process before Demo Path and capture planning.

availableCommands.name: $browser:control-in-app-browser
availableCommands.description: Control the in-app Browser for opening, navigating, inspecting visible or interactive page state, clicking, typing, screenshots, and local web testing. It can have existing signed-in sessions. For semantic operations on linked resources, prefer a purpose-built connector, API, or CLI when available.

availableCommands.name: $build-macos-apps:appkit-interop
availableCommands.description: Bridge macOS SwiftUI into AppKit narrowly. Use when implementing representables, reaching NSWindow or panels, handling menus, or using the responder chain.

availableCommands.name: $build-macos-apps:build-run-debug
availableCommands.description: Build, run, and debug macOS apps with shell-first Xcode and Swift workflows. Use when launching apps or diagnosing build, startup, or runtime failures.

availableCommands.name: $build-macos-apps:liquid-glass
availableCommands.description: Implement and review macOS SwiftUI Liquid Glass UI. Use when adopting system glass, removing conflicting custom chrome, or building glass surfaces.

availableCommands.name: $build-macos-apps:packaging-notarization
availableCommands.description: Prepare macOS packaging and notarization workflows. Use when archiving apps, validating bundles, or explaining distribution-only failures.

availableCommands.name: $build-macos-apps:signing-entitlements
availableCommands.description: Inspect macOS signing, entitlements, and Gatekeeper issues. Use when diagnosing code signing, sandbox, hardened runtime, or trust failures.

availableCommands.name: $build-macos-apps:swiftpm-macos
availableCommands.description: Build, run, and test SwiftPM macOS packages and executables. Use when the repo is package-first or has no Xcode project.

availableCommands.name: $build-macos-apps:swiftui-patterns
availableCommands.description: Build macOS SwiftUI scenes and components with desktop patterns. Use when shaping windows, commands, toolbars, settings, split views, or inspectors.

availableCommands.name: $build-macos-apps:telemetry
availableCommands.description: Add and verify lightweight macOS runtime telemetry. Use when wiring Logger events or inspecting logs for windows, sidebars, menus, and actions.

availableCommands.name: $build-macos-apps:test-triage
availableCommands.description: Triage macOS tests across Xcode and SwiftPM. Use when narrowing failures, explaining assertions or crashes, or separating setup from regressions.

availableCommands.name: $build-macos-apps:view-refactor
availableCommands.description: Refactor macOS SwiftUI views and scenes into stable structure. Use when splitting large views, tightening scene state, or narrowing AppKit escapes.

availableCommands.name: $build-macos-apps:window-management
availableCommands.description: Customize macOS SwiftUI windows and scene behavior. Use when tuning window chrome, drag regions, placement, restoration, launch behavior, or borderless windows.

availableCommands.name: $chrome:control-chrome
availableCommands.description: Control the user's Chrome browser for tasks that depend on existing Chrome state: tabs, logged-in sessions, or extensions. Prefer purpose-built connectors, APIs, or CLIs when available.

availableCommands.name: $computer-use:computer-use
availableCommands.description: Control local Mac apps through Computer Use for tasks that require reading or operating app UI. Prefer purpose-built connectors, APIs, or CLIs when available.

availableCommands.name: $documents:documents
availableCommands.description: Create, edit, redline, and comment on `.docx`, Word, and Google Docs-targeted document artifacts inside the container, with a strict render-and-verify workflow. Use `render_docx.py` to generate page PNGs (and optional PDF) for visual QA, then iterate until layout is flawless before delivering the final document.

availableCommands.name: $effect-ts
availableCommands.description: Use this skill whenever working in a repository that uses Effect, even if the current task is in a new file or the user does not explicitly ask for Effect help. Apply it to any work that should follow the repository's Effect patterns, conventions, architecture, or supporting tooling. Also use it for questions about Effect patterns, services, layers, schemas, streams, runtimes, or typed error handling.

availableCommands.name: $github:gh-address-comments
availableCommands.description: Address actionable GitHub pull request review feedback. Use when the user wants to inspect unresolved review threads, requested changes, or inline review comments on a PR, then implement selected fixes. Use the GitHub app for PR metadata and flat comment reads, and use the bundled GraphQL script via `gh` whenever thread-level state, resolution status, or inline review context matters.

availableCommands.name: $github:gh-fix-ci
availableCommands.description: Use when a user asks to debug or fix failing GitHub PR checks that run in GitHub Actions. Use the GitHub app from this plugin for PR metadata and patch context, and use `gh` for Actions check and log inspection before implementing any approved fix.

availableCommands.name: $github:github
availableCommands.description: Triage and orient GitHub repository, pull request, and issue work through the connected GitHub app. Use when the user asks for general GitHub help, wants PR or issue summaries, or needs repository context before choosing a more specific GitHub workflow.

availableCommands.name: $github:yeet
availableCommands.description: Publish local changes to GitHub by confirming scope, committing intentionally, pushing the branch, and opening a draft PR through the GitHub app from this plugin, with `gh` used only as a fallback where connector coverage is insufficient.

availableCommands.name: $gmail:gmail
availableCommands.description: Manage Gmail inbox triage, mailbox search, thread summaries, action extraction, reply drafting, and email forwarding through connected Gmail data. Use when the user wants to inspect a mailbox or thread, search email with Gmail query syntax, summarize messages, extract decisions and follow-ups, prepare replies or forwarded messages, or organize messages with explicit confirmation before send, archive, delete, or label actions.

availableCommands.name: $gmail:gmail-inbox-triage
availableCommands.description: Triage a Gmail inbox into actionable buckets such as urgent, needs reply soon, waiting, and FYI using connected Gmail data. Use when the user asks to triage the inbox, rank what needs attention, find what still needs a reply, or separate important mail from noise.

availableCommands.name: $google-drive:google-docs
availableCommands.description: Connector-first Google Docs creation and editing in local Codex plugin sessions, with direct native create and batchUpdate workflows for simple docs, DOCX-first import for polished deliverables, target-document checks, smart chip and building-block reconstruction, connector-readback verification, and reference routing for formatting, citations, tables, and write-safety.

availableCommands.name: $google-drive:google-drive
availableCommands.description: Use connected Google Drive as the single entrypoint for Drive, Docs, Sheets, and Slides work. Use when the user wants to find, fetch, organize, share, export, copy, or delete Drive files, or summarize and edit Google Docs, Google Sheets, and Google Slides through one unified Google Drive plugin.

availableCommands.name: $google-drive:google-drive-comments
availableCommands.description: Write, reply to, and resolve Google Drive comments on Docs, Sheets, Slides, and Drive files with evidence-backed location context. Use when the user asks to leave comments, review a file with comments, respond to comment threads, or resolve Drive comments.

availableCommands.name: $google-drive:google-sheets
availableCommands.description: Analyze and edit connected Google Sheets with range precision. Use when the user wants to create Google Sheets, find a spreadsheet, inspect tabs or ranges, search rows, plan formulas, create or repair charts, clean or restructure tables, write concise summaries, or make explicit cell-range updates.

availableCommands.name: $google-drive:google-slides
availableCommands.description: Google Slides work for finding, reading, summarizing, creating, importing, template following, visual cleanup, source-deck adaptation, structural repair, and content edits in native Slides decks.

availableCommands.name: $migrate-to-codex
availableCommands.description: Migrate supported instruction files, skills, agents, and MCP config into Codex project and global files.

availableCommands.name: $notion:notion-knowledge-capture
availableCommands.description: Capture conversations into structured Notion pages

availableCommands.name: $notion:notion-meeting-intelligence
availableCommands.description: Prep meetings with Notion context and tailored agendas

availableCommands.name: $notion:notion-research-documentation
availableCommands.description: Research Notion content and produce briefs/reports

availableCommands.name: $notion:notion-spec-to-implementation
availableCommands.description: Turn Notion specs into implementation plans, tasks, and progress tracking

availableCommands.name: $openai-templates:artifact-template-analytics-dashboard
availableCommands.description: Create a spreadsheet using the Analytics Dashboard template and its retained reference file. Use when the user selects or names Analytics Dashboard. Monitor acquisition, engagement, retention, revenue, and conversion funnel KPIs with charts.

availableCommands.name: $openai-templates:artifact-template-business-review
availableCommands.description: Create a presentation using the Business Review template and its retained reference file. Use when the user selects or names Business Review. Review business performance, KPIs, segment results, strategic priorities, decisions, and outlook.

availableCommands.name: $openai-templates:artifact-template-design-report
availableCommands.description: Create a document using the Design Report template and its retained reference file. Use when the user selects or names Design Report. Produce design reports with an executive summary, key findings, implications, recommendations, and appendix.

availableCommands.name: $openai-templates:artifact-template-experiment-analysis
availableCommands.description: Create a document using the Experiment Analysis template and its retained reference file. Use when the user selects or names Experiment Analysis. Analyze experiments with hypotheses, methodology, results, interpretation, limitations, and next steps.

availableCommands.name: $openai-templates:artifact-template-financial-budget
availableCommands.description: Create a spreadsheet using the Financial Budget template and its retained reference file. Use when the user selects or names Financial Budget. Model actuals, budget and scenario forecasts, variances, cash runway, and departmental plans.

availableCommands.name: $openai-templates:artifact-template-investment-committee-memo
availableCommands.description: Create a document using the Investment Committee Memo template and its retained reference file. Use when the user selects or names Investment Committee Memo. Prepare investment committee memos with the thesis, transaction details, financial analysis, risks, and recommendation.

availableCommands.name: $openai-templates:artifact-template-legal-memorandum
availableCommands.description: Create a document using the Legal Memorandum template and its retained reference file. Use when the user selects or names Legal Memorandum. Draft legal memoranda with the issue, brief answer, relevant facts, analysis, and conclusion.

availableCommands.name: $openai-templates:artifact-template-market-trends-report
availableCommands.description: Create a presentation using the Market Trends Report template and its retained reference file. Use when the user selects or names Market Trends Report. Communicate market or industry trends, supporting evidence, implications, and recommended responses.

availableCommands.name: $openai-templates:artifact-template-minimal-letterhead
availableCommands.description: Create a document using the Minimal Letterhead template and its retained reference file. Use when the user selects or names Minimal Letterhead. Write professional business letters with sender, recipient, message, and signature fields in a minimal letterhead layout.

availableCommands.name: $openai-templates:artifact-template-operating-calendar
availableCommands.description: Create a spreadsheet using the Operating Calendar template and its retained reference file. Use when the user selects or names Operating Calendar. Plan annual and monthly operating milestones, campaigns, launches, deadlines, and recurring events.

availableCommands.name: $openai-templates:artifact-template-operating-review
availableCommands.description: Create a presentation using the Operating Review template and its retained reference file. Use when the user selects or names Operating Review. Run weekly operating reviews with scorecards, functional updates, risks, decisions, and action items.

availableCommands.name: $openai-templates:artifact-template-project-kickoff
availableCommands.description: Create a presentation using the Project Kickoff template and its retained reference file. Use when the user selects or names Project Kickoff. Align teams on project goals, scope, roles, milestones, risks, and the working model.

availableCommands.name: $openai-templates:artifact-template-project-tracker
availableCommands.description: Create a spreadsheet using the Project Tracker template and its retained reference file. Use when the user selects or names Project Tracker. Manage workstreams, tasks, owners, status, priority, dates, launch pulse, and a Gantt schedule.

availableCommands.name: $openai-templates:artifact-template-sales-pipeline
availableCommands.description: Create a spreadsheet using the Sales Pipeline template and its retained reference file. Use when the user selects or names Sales Pipeline. Track opportunities, stages, owners, deal sizes, probabilities, forecasts, next steps, and risks.

availableCommands.name: $openai-templates:artifact-template-simple-dark-mode
availableCommands.description: Create a presentation using the Simple Dark Mode template and its retained reference file. Use when the user selects or names Simple Dark Mode. Create clean dark-mode presentations with bold typography, simple sections, charts, and imagery.

availableCommands.name: $openai-templates:artifact-template-simple-light-mode
availableCommands.description: Create a presentation using the Simple Light Mode template and its retained reference file. Use when the user selects or names Simple Light Mode. Create clean light-mode presentations with spacious typography, simple sections, charts, and imagery.

availableCommands.name: $openai-templates:artifact-template-strategy-memorandum
availableCommands.description: Create a document using the Strategy Memorandum template and its retained reference file. Use when the user selects or names Strategy Memorandum. Present strategic context, choices, rationale, risks, milestones, and a clear recommendation.

availableCommands.name: $openai-templates:artifact-template-system-design
availableCommands.description: Create a document using the System Design template and its retained reference file. Use when the user selects or names System Design. Document system architecture, requirements, components, data flows, APIs, tradeoffs, and operational considerations.

availableCommands.name: $openai-templates:artifact-template-team-alignment
availableCommands.description: Create a presentation using the Team Alignment template and its retained reference file. Use when the user selects or names Team Alignment. Facilitate team offsites and planning with context, goals, priorities, decisions, and action items.

availableCommands.name: $openai-templates:artifact-template-three-statement-forecast
availableCommands.description: Create a spreadsheet using the Three-Statement Forecast template and its retained reference file. Use when the user selects or names Three-Statement Forecast. Build an integrated income statement, balance sheet, and cash flow forecast with assumptions, checks, and an executive summary.

availableCommands.name: $pdf:pdf
availableCommands.description: Read, create, inspect, render, and verify PDF files where visual layout matters. Use Poppler rendering plus Python tools such as reportlab, pdfplumber, and pypdf for generation and extraction.

availableCommands.name: $presentations:Presentations
availableCommands.description: Create or edit PowerPoint or Google Slides decks

availableCommands.name: $simplify-code
availableCommands.description: Review and clean up changed code

availableCommands.name: $sites:sites-building
availableCommands.description: Use Sites to build websites, including landing pages, portfolios, dashboards, portals, trackers, hubs, and internal tools. Always use Sites when the project contains `.openai/hosting.json`.

availableCommands.name: $sites:sites-hosting
availableCommands.description: Host websites with Sites. Always use after `sites-building`, and use for website publishing, deployment, hosting management, or projects containing `.openai/hosting.json`.

availableCommands.name: $spreadsheets:Spreadsheets
availableCommands.description: Create, edit, analyze, and verify standalone spreadsheet files or Google Sheets-ready workbooks, including .xlsx, .xls, .csv, and .tsv. Do not use for live controlling Microsoft Excel app or a live Excel session.

availableCommands.name: $spreadsheets:excel-live-control
availableCommands.description: Control an open or active Microsoft Excel workbook through the ChatGPT add-in or connected session. Use when the user tags the Microsoft Excel app in Codex or follows up on an established live Excel task. Do not use for standalone spreadsheet files or Google Sheets.

availableCommands.name: $template-creator:template-creator
availableCommands.description: Create or update a reusable personal Codex artifact-template skill. Use when the user invokes $template-creator or asks in natural language to create a template using, from, or based on an attached Word document, PowerPoint presentation, or Excel workbook, or explicitly asks to edit or update a passed artifact-template skill. Do not use for one-off artifact creation from an existing template.

availableCommands.name: $use-railway
availableCommands.description: Operate Railway infrastructure: create projects, provision services and databases, manage object storage buckets, deploy code, configure environments and variables, manage domains, troubleshoot failures, check status and metrics, set up Railway agent tooling, and query Railway docs. Use this skill whenever the user mentions Railway, deployments, services, environments, buckets, object storage, build failures, agent setup, MCP, or infrastructure operations, even if they don't say "Railway" explicitly.

availableCommands.name: $visualize:visualize
availableCommands.description: Create visualizations and interactive tools in conversation. Use when asked to show how something works, make simulators or labs, maps, plots, charts or graphs, comparisons, scenarios, adjustable inputs, and exploration.

availableCommands.name: $imagegen
availableCommands.description: Generate or edit raster images when the task benefits from AI-created bitmap visuals such as photos, illustrations, textures, sprites, mockups, or transparent-background cutouts. Use when Codex should create a brand-new image, transform an existing image, or derive visual variants from references, and the output should be a bitmap asset rather than repo-native code or vector. Do not use when the task is better handled by editing existing SVG/vector/code-native assets, extending an established icon or logo system, or building the visual directly in HTML/CSS/canvas.

availableCommands.name: $openai-docs
availableCommands.description: Use when the user asks how to build with OpenAI products or APIs, asks about Codex itself or choosing Codex surfaces, needs up-to-date official documentation with citations, help choosing the latest model for a use case, or model upgrade and prompt-upgrade guidance; use OpenAI docs MCP tools for non-Codex docs questions, use the Codex manual helper first for broad Codex self-knowledge, and restrict fallback browsing to official OpenAI domains.

availableCommands.name: $plugin-creator
availableCommands.description: Create and scaffold plugin directories for Codex with a required `.codex-plugin/plugin.json`, optional plugin folders/files, valid manifest defaults, and personal-marketplace entries by default. Use when Codex needs to create a new personal plugin, add optional plugin structure, generate or update marketplace entries for plugin ordering and availability metadata, or update an existing local plugin during development with the CLI-driven cachebuster and reinstall flow.

availableCommands.name: $skill-creator
availableCommands.description: Create or update a skill

availableCommands.name: $skill-installer
availableCommands.description: Install curated skills from openai/skills or other repos

rawOutput.formatted_output:
---
name: william-draft-skill-md
description: Drafts or revises a Skill Bundle's output/SKILL.md from its design.md. Use when handed a bundle's design.md (and optionally prior review revise notes) and asked to produce or update output/SKILL.md.
---

You are drafting `output/SKILL.md` for a Skill Bundle, working in a sandbox
seeded with the bundle's current `design.md` (and `output/SKILL.md`, if one
already exists). Follow these steps in order.

1. **Read `design.md` in the current directory.**

   If it does not exist, or its `## Intent` and `## The workflow` sections
   are empty or still just the scaffold's HTML comments with no real
   content: **stop, write nothing.** Do not create `output/SKILL.md`. End
   your final message with a plain statement that `design.md` doesn't have
   enough content yet to draft a SKILL.md. A fabricated skill is worse than
   no skill.

2. **Check your prompt for a "REVISE NOTES:" section.**

   If present, a human reviewer already looked at a previous
   `output/SKILL.md` draft and is asking for something specific. Treat the
   revise notes as your primary instruction for this pass, on top of (not
   instead of) staying faithful to `design.md`.

3. **Check whether `output/SKILL.md` already exists.**

   If it does, treat it as a first draft to revise, not something to
   discard. Preserve any part that still matches `design.md`'s current
   `## Intent` / `## The workflow`. Rewrite only what has drifted, or what
   the revise notes call out.

4. **Write (or update) `output/SKILL.md`:**

   - **Frontmatter**: `name` (the bundle's slug, kebab-case, from
     `design.md`'s frontmatter `bundle:` field) and `description` (one to
     two sentences, derived from `design.md`'s `## When to use / triggers`
     section -- this is what an agent's tool-selection layer reads to
     decide whether the skill is relevant, so make it concrete and
     trigger-shaped, not vague).
   - **Body**: direct, second-person instructions to the agent that will
     run this skill -- write *to* that agent, not *about* the skill.
     Translate `design.md`'s `## The workflow` into a concrete numbered
     procedure. Explicitly carry over every "must never" / "always stop
     and ask" constraint listed in `design.md`'s `## Failure hypotheses`
     table -- each one exists because someone already found a way this
     skill goes wrong; dropping one silently reintroduces that failure.
   - Keep it as short as it can be while remaining unambiguous. This file
     will be read by an agent under token pressure, not skimmed by a human
     -- prefer a tight numbered procedure over prose.

5. **Stay in scope.** Only create or edit `design.md` and
   `output/SKILL.md`. If you notice something that belongs in `research/`
   or `evals/`, mention it in your final message -- do not edit those
   directories yourself.

6. **Stop once `output/SKILL.md` reflects `design.md`.** Do not run the
   drafted skill, do not write eval fixtures, do not change the bundle's
   stage. Those are separate, human-gated steps.

--- DESIGN ---
---
bundle: add-license-header
---
# Design — Add License Header

## Intent

A file-hygiene skill for a codebase that wants a consistent SPDX license
header on every source file. It exists so contributors (human or agent)
never have to remember the exact header text or forget to add it on new
files -- the skill knows the one canonical header this project uses and
applies it exactly.

## When to use / triggers

Use this skill when asked to add a license header to a file, or to a
directory of files, that does not already have one -- e.g. "add the license
header to src/foo.ts" or "make sure every file in src/ has our license
header."

## The workflow

1. Determine the target file(s) from the request (a single path, or every
   file under a given directory matching the project's source extensions).
2. For each target file, read its first 20 lines and check whether an SPDX
   license header is already present (look for the string
   `SPDX-License-Identifier`).
3. If a header is already present, skip that file -- do not add a second
   header.
4. If no header is present, insert this exact two-line header at the very
   top of the file, above any existing content:
   ```
   // SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
   // Copyright (c) 2026 Acme Corp
   ```
5. Report which files were changed and which were skipped (already had a
   header).

## Failure hypotheses

| # | How it could fail | Risk family |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | The skill adds a second header to a file that already has one, instead of skipping it | OUT |
| 2 | The skill inserts the header after existing content (e.g. after a shebang or existing comment) instead of at the very top | OUT |

name: william-draft-skill-md
description: Drafts or revises a Skill Bundle's output/SKILL.md from its design.md. Use when handed a bundle's design.md (and optionally prior review revise notes) and asked to produce or update output/SKILL.md.
---

You are drafting `output/SKILL.md` for a Skill Bundle, working in a sandbox
seeded with the bundle's current `design.md` (and `output/SKILL.md`, if one
already exists). Follow these steps in order.

1. **Read `design.md` in the current directory.**

   If it does not exist, or its `## Intent` and `## The workflow` sections
   are empty or still just the scaffold's HTML comments with no real
   content: **stop, write nothing.** Do not create `output/SKILL.md`. End
   your final message with a plain statement that `design.md` doesn't have
   enough content yet to draft a SKILL.md. A fabricated skill is worse than
   no skill.

2. **Check your prompt for a "REVISE NOTES:" section.**

update.title:
if test -e output/SKILL.md; then sed -n '1,260p' output/SKILL.md; else printf 'NO_EXISTING_SKILL\n'; fi
find . -maxdepth 2 -type f -print | sort

content.newText:
---
name: add-license-header
description: Add the project's canonical SPDX license header to a requested source file or to source files under a requested directory. Use when asked to add or ensure license headers on files that may not already have one.
---

# Add License Header

1. Determine the target files from the request:
   - For a file path, target that file.
   - For a directory, target every file beneath it whose extension matches the project's source extensions.
2. Read the first 20 lines of every target file and look for `SPDX-License-Identifier`.
3. If that string is present, skip the file. Never add a second SPDX license header.
4. Otherwise, insert these exact lines at the very top of the file, before all existing content:

   ```text
   // SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
   // Copyright (c) 2026 Acme Corp
   ```

   Always put the header at the absolute beginning; never place it after a shebang, comment, or any other existing content.
5. Report the files changed and the files skipped because they already had a header.

update.title:
sed -n '1,240p' output/SKILL.md
git status --short
git diff -- design.md output/SKILL.md

rawInput.command:
sed -n '1,240p' output/SKILL.md
git status --short
git diff -- design.md output/SKILL.md
